tier_intro: This is the one that makes the biggest immediate difference. How does AI talk TO you? Direct or careful? Short or detailed? With humor or straight? 3-5 minutes and your AI suddenly feels like it knows you.

tier_complete.headline: Communication profile built.
tier_complete.body: AI now knows exactly how to talk to you — the format, the tone, the level of directness. This alone eliminates 80% of 'ugh, not again' moments. Next: how you think and process information.

questions.question: You ask AI something and get a 6-paragraph response when you wanted a quick answer. Your reaction?

options.label: Mildly annoyed — I just skim to the actual answer
Genuinely frustrated — this is the #1 thing that bothers me
options.description: I want the answer, not an essay

options.label: Depends on the question — complex topics deserve more
Fine with it — I like seeing the full reasoning

questions.meta_profiling: How long is their own response to this question? Short = minimal preference validated. Note if they justify their choice extensively — ironic signal of verbosity comfort.
questions.why_this_matters: Calibrates default response length across all AI exports.

questions.question: What's your ideal default response style?

options.label: 1-3 lines for simple things, expand only if asked
Bullet points by default, no preamble
Short paragraphs, max 5-7 lines

questions.question: A friend texts you: 'Just give it to me straight, I can handle it.' That phrase feels...

options.label: Exactly how I'd say it myself
Relatable — I generally prefer directness
Depends — direct for facts, softer for emotional stuff
I prefer tact — directness without warmth feels harsh

questions.meta_profiling: How they answer — length, whether they add caveats ('it depends on...') — is itself a directness signal. Short pick = direct. Long explanation = diplomatic.
questions.why_this_matters: Shapes how AI delivers assessments, feedback, and bad news.

questions.question: When AI explains something, what format makes it land best for you?
questions.instruction: Drag to rank from most to least useful (or just pick your top 2)

options.label: Bullet points
Numbered steps
Flowing prose
Tables / comparisons
Code / technical blocks

questions.meta_profiling: If user selects prose AND bullets — note format_preference as adaptive. Code blocks selection correlates with tech_comfort >= 7.
questions.why_this_matters: AI defaults to your preferred structure instead of whatever it feels like.

questions.question: Emojis in AI responses — your honest take?

options.label: Please no. They make it feel fake and childish.
Occasionally fine — just not every sentence
I don't really notice them either way
I like them — adds personality and clarity

questions.meta_profiling: Did they use any emojis in their own answers during profiling? Cross-reference with stated preference. Incongruence = note.
questions.why_this_matters: Sounds trivial, but it's one of the top complaints about AI tone.

questions.question: AI just finished explaining something and ends with 'Let me know if you'd like me to elaborate or if you have any questions!' — you feel...

options.label: This is exactly the filler I want gone
options.description: If I want more, I'll ask

options.label: Mildly annoying, but not a dealbreaker
Fine — helpful reminder that I can continue
Honestly don't notice it

questions.meta_profiling: Strong 'filler_hate' signal compounds with verbosity_preference minimal — double-flag for 'no outro lines' instruction.
questions.why_this_matters: Removes AI habits that are technically polite but feel hollow to you specifically.

questions.question: You ask a complex question. AI gives you the short answer first, then says 'want me to go deeper?' vs AI gives you the full breakdown immediately. Which do you prefer?

options.label: Short answer first, I'll ask for more if I want it
Full breakdown immediately — I hate going back and forth
Depends on the type of question

questions.why_this_matters: AI learns whether to give you the headline or the whole story by default.

questions.question: You've just done something — shipped a project, finished a hard task. AI says 'Great job, you should be really proud!' Your reaction?

options.label: Cringe. Skip the cheerleading, please.
Neutral acknowledgment is fine, excessive praise isn't
Honestly? I don't mind it. Feels encouraging.
Give me ALL the affirmations

questions.meta_profiling: Cross-reference with validation_need in Tier 4. If high validation need but 'cringe' here — note the contradiction: wants acknowledgment but from peers not tools.
questions.why_this_matters: AI knows whether to celebrate with you or just acknowledge and move on.

questions.question: Humor in AI conversations — where do you sit?

options.label: Professional mode only — keep it focused
Dry wit or clever observations — yes please
Light humor here and there — keeps things enjoyable
Full personality — I want AI to actually be fun

questions.meta_profiling: If they write a funny answer themselves — validate high humor preference even if stated 'none'.
questions.why_this_matters: Calibrates AI personality from strict assistant to genuinely enjoyable collaborator.

questions.question: You show AI your work and ask for feedback. You want it to...

options.label: Be brutal — I don't need hand-holding, I need the truth
Honest and direct — what's broken and how to fix it
The full picture — what works, what doesn't, specific improvements
Lead with what's good first, then the improvements

questions.meta_profiling: Feedback style preference is one of the highest-signal dimensions for cross-domain AI use. Note this carefully. First choice speed = confidence in preference.
questions.why_this_matters: How AI approaches anything you share for review — writing, code, ideas, plans.

questions.question: You're asking about something technical you half-know. AI uses precise technical jargon vs everyday language. Your preference?

options.label: Hit me with proper terminology — I can look up what I don't know
Use the right terms but explain them if obscure
Plain language please — translate the jargon for me
Analogies and examples — make it concrete

questions.meta_profiling: Compare with tech_comfort from Tier 0. Mismatches (low tech comfort + 'full jargon') may indicate domain expertise in non-technical fields. Note for domain-specific adaptation.
questions.why_this_matters: AI calibrates vocabulary to your level — no oversimplification, no unexplained acronyms.

questions.question: AI is working through something complex and explains its reasoning step-by-step as it goes. You...

options.label: Love it — I want to follow the logic, not just get the conclusion
Just show me the conclusion — hide the working
Show me the key decision points, skip the obvious steps
For important decisions yes, for quick tasks no

questions.meta_profiling: Thinking-aloud preference separates people who want AI as a transparent collaborator from those who want a clean results machine. High interest in reasoning often correlates with analytical decision style.
questions.why_this_matters: Sets whether AI shows its work or just delivers the answer.

questions.question: AI says 'This might work, but I'm not 100% certain' vs AI says 'Do this.' When the answer is genuinely uncertain, you prefer...

options.label: Always flag uncertainty — I'd rather know than get false confidence
Give me a confident answer even if uncertain — I'll verify what matters
Flag it only when the stakes are high — low stakes, just answer
Don't hedge — give me 2 options and let me pick

questions.meta_profiling: Hedging tolerance is a trust-calibration dimension. Someone who hates hedging may actually be building blind trust — note. Someone who demands hedging in all cases may have been burned before.
questions.why_this_matters: Determines how AI communicates its own uncertainty — whether to say 'probably' or just commit.

questions.question: AI is about to tell you something you might not want to hear. You'd prefer it...

options.label: Just say it. Straight up, no padding.
Give me context so I understand WHY before the verdict
Acknowledge what I was going for first, then the reality
Lead with the problem and immediately give me a path forward

questions.meta_profiling: Cross-reference with directness preference (t1_q02) and feedback_style (t1_q09). Consistent 'direct' across all three = very high directness. Inconsistent = context-sensitive.
questions.why_this_matters: Shapes how AI handles hard truths — when your idea has a flaw, when a plan won't work.

questions.question: Do you ever switch between languages mid-conversation? (like writing in one language but dropping terms from another)

options.label: Yes — I naturally mix languages, AI should follow along
Sometimes — mostly technical terms stay in English even if I write in another language
No — I stick to one language per conversation

questions.meta_profiling: If language from Tier 0 is non-English but answer here is 'technical_only' — note bilingual pattern for export: respond in native language, keep technical terms in English.
questions.why_this_matters: AI can mirror your natural multilingual flow instead of staying rigidly in one language.

questions.question: Which languages do you typically mix?
You receive a message where the tone is playfully sarcastic: 'Oh sure, that'll definitely work perfectly.' Your immediate read?

options.label: Got it immediately — sarcasm is clear to me in text
Usually catch it, but sometimes I need a moment
Honestly? I sometimes take it literally, especially in text
I get it, but I find sarcasm in text annoying — be direct

questions.meta_profiling: Low sarcasm comprehension = AI should avoid ironic or sarcastic framing entirely. Dislikes = same outcome. High + humor preference = AI can use gentle sarcasm.
questions.why_this_matters: Prevents AI from being accidentally confusing or condescending through tone that doesn't land in text.

questions.question: AI remembers something you mentioned in passing three weeks ago and brings it up now. You feel...

options.label: Impressed — that's exactly the kind of continuity I want
Slightly unsettled — I forgot I said that
Depends what it was — relevant = great, random = weird
I'd rather start fresh each time — don't accumulate stuff about me

questions.meta_profiling: Memory appreciation signals privacy comfort level and desire for continuity. Cautious or minimal = AI should not surface old details without clear relevance. High = AI should proactively connect threads.
questions.why_this_matters: Sets how aggressively AI cross-references things you've shared in the past.

questions.question: When you're in the middle of explaining something and AI jumps in with an answer before you've finished — you feel:

options.label: Annoyed — I wasn't done. Let me finish my thought.
Fine actually — if it got the gist, time saved
Fine if it got it right, annoying if it misunderstood

questions.why_this_matters: Shapes whether AI completes your thought or waits for full context before responding.

questions.question: You're reviewing code that someone else wrote. Comments in the code — your preference?

options.label: Clean code shouldn't need comments — names should explain everything
Comment the WHY not the WHAT — I can read the code
Thorough comments — good for onboarding and future me
Depends on complexity — obvious code = none, tricky logic = comment

questions.meta_profiling: Code comment style is a proxy for documentation philosophy generally. Thorough comments = likely same person who likes detailed AI explanations.
questions.why_this_matters: AI matches your documentation philosophy when generating or reviewing code.
